pi_items.source: Data characteristics analysis: (1) No missing values (unrealistic for real data); (2) Discount values form exact 0.01 increments from 0.0-0.3; (3) Regions are generic cardinal directions; (4) Sales reps are generic first names; (5) Product categories are generic; (6) Date range exactly 2023-01-01 to 2024-01-01 (exactly 1 year); (7) Product IDs sequential 1001-1020; (8) All categorical values appear pre-selected and evenly distributed

Task complexity assessment: Creating a clustered bar chart in Power BI Desktop is a basic operation typically achievable by beginners in 2-5 minutes. No advanced DAX, complex modeling, or multi-step transformations required.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic sales dataset with exactly 1000 rows. The CSV file is named 'sales_data.csv' and contains columns for Product_ID, Sale_Date, Sales_Rep, Region, Sales_Amount, Quantity_Sold, Product_Category, Unit_Cost, Unit_Price, Customer_Type, Discount, Payment_Method, Sales_Channel, and an additional undocumented column 'Region_and_Sales_Rep'. The data is synthetic with artificial characteristics: no missing values, sequential product IDs (1001-1020), generic region names (East, North, South, West), generic sales rep names (Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, Eve), and discount values in exact 0.01 increments from 0.0-0.3. The date range covers exactly 2023-01-01 to 2024-01-01. The task is classified as 'easy' difficulty and requires creating a clustered bar chart with Region on Y-axis and sum of Sales_Amount on X-axis, then saving as 'sales_report.pbix'.
